stories.title: Chennai Love Story movie review: 7 tweets you must read before watching Abbavaram, Sri Gouri Priya's Telug
stories.summary: The wait is over! The Telugu romantic film Chennai Love Story finally hit theatres on July 24. Directed by Ravi Namburii, the movie stars Kiran Abbavaram and Sri Gouri Priya in the lead roles. The film features music composed by Mani Sharma and has been produced by SKN and Sai Rajesh under the banners of Amrutha Productions and Mass Movie Makers. Fans rushed to theatres for the first-day-first-show, and the first reactions are now pouring in on social media. Netizens review Chennai Love StoryThe romantic drama had generated considerable buzz before its release, thanks to its promotional campaign and a pre-release event attended by Megastar Chiranjeevi.

stories.title: Arsenal set to step up Guimaraes pursuit as Newcastle expect bid
stories.summary: The Gunners have admired the 28-year-old for several years, stretching back even before he moved to Tyneside from Lyon in January 2022. Standard Sport understands that official talks between Arsenal and Newcastle have not yet taken place. Newcastle sources are adamant that their Premier League rivals have not yet approached them to make an offer for Guimaraes. The Magpies also insist they will not be pushed into selling their skipper and remain determined to keep hold of him. However, Arsenal are now expected to accelerate their interest in Guimaraes and push to bring him to north London.

stories.title: Why Leeds want James Trafford – and why he would be such a key signing
stories.summary: Leeds United reached 100 points en route to their 2024-25 Championship title. In most other campaigns, that huge tally would have put them way beyond every other club in the division. As it was, they only won the league on goal difference. Burnley finished second, but they too hit a century of points from the 46 matches. Leeds’ 95 goals dwarfed Burnley’s 69, but the latter were able to keep pace with their rivals because of their stingy defence. Burnley, barely believably, conceded 16 goals in the 46 games. That total was only one higher than Chelsea’s record for the fewest goals conceded in an English football season (15).

stories.title: Avatar: Seven Havens Officially Reveals Korra's Earthbender Successor With October 9 Premiere
stories.summary: Published Jul 23, 2026, 6:06 PM EDT Leo Reyna is an entertainment journalist and editor with extensive experience covering anime, manga, and gaming. Before joining CBR, he contributed to major entertainment outlets including Static Media and Ranker, where he developed a reputation for insightful industry coverage and a deep understanding of fan culture. At CBR, Leo reports on key developments within the anime industry—examining topics such as production challenges, studio practices, and creative communities—while also spotlighting the passion and artistry of fandoms worldwide.

stories.title: Love Island SPOILER: 'Gameplay' accusations rock the villa as Maya Jama puts Islanders in the hot seat
stories.summary: By on 23rd July 2026 in By on 23rd July 2026 in The events of The Grafties have boiled over into another episode of Love Island , as accusations of 'gameplay' have unsettled the Islanders. In a first look at Thursday's episode, Priya questions Julia over her thoughts on her connection with Aidan. She asks: 'Do you think I’m genuinely with Aidan as a gameplay, to get to the final? ' Julia admits: 'I do think you’ve developed a connection. He would pick you over any new bombshell as that would guarantee the final. ' Priya and Yasmin, Love Island. Pic: ITV The conversation shifts to the wider dynamics in the Villa.

stories.title: Live updates: US says it hit Iranian military targets in 13th consecutive night of strikes
stories.summary: Live Updates Video Ad Feedback Wife of US service member killed during Iran war shares photos, memories of him 1:47 • Source: CNN Wife of US service member killed during Iran war shares photos, memories of him 1:47 Ad Feedback • No respite from war: The US military said it completed a 13th consecutive night of strikes against Iran. The latest onslaught came hours after President Donald Trump signaled he’s not yet ready to negotiate a new ceasefire with Tehran.

stories.title: Beloved gas station chain reveals managers can earn up to $275K without college degree
stories.summary: Buc-ee's is fueling debate online after a sign displaying employee pay rates revealed that some store managers can earn as much as $275,000 a year without a college degree. The pay scale, which has circulated widely on social media, lists cashiers, warehouse workers, maintenance employees and grocery stockers starting at $20 an hour. Food service managers can earn between $150,000 and $200,000 annually, while general managers can make between $200,000 and $275,000.

stories.title: Miquita Oliver apologises to Fearne Cotton over past jibe
stories.summary: Miquita Oliver apologises to Fearne Cotton over 'awful' comment in the past Updated / Wednesday, 22 Jul 2026 18:15 Getty (L-R) Miquita Oliver and Fearne Cotton - Oliver was let go from her radio job in 2005 following an interview with NME where she was asked how she would improve Top of the Pops and replied that the programme should get rid of Cotton Presenter Miquita Oliver has apologised to Fearne Cotton after Oliver was sacked from BBC Radio 1 in 2005 for making an "awful" comment about her fellow broadcaster.

stories.title: US House passes war powers resolution directing Trump to end Iran conflict
stories.summary: The US House of Representatives for the second time passed a war powers resolution directing Donald Trump to withdraw the US military from the conflict with Iran, amid renewed fighting that has driven oil prices to their highest levels in weeks and claimed four American lives. The resolution proposed by Democrat Pramila Jayapal passed by a 214-208 vote, largely along party lines. The same four Republicans who had helped pass a nearly identical resolution in June – Tom Barrett of Michigan, Warren Davidson of Ohio, Thomas Massie of Kentucky and Brian Fitzpatrick of Pennsylvania – joined with all Democrats in attendance to vote in favor.

stories.title: Fortnite Cube Sprites Release Date Explained
stories.summary: What are the Fortnite Cube Sprites? This is a new feature that we’re expecting to arrive in the next Fortnite update. It’ll be a brand-new variant for each of the sprites. joining Holofoil, Gold, and Galaxy as a new type of collectible. But how exactly will they work? Chapter 7 Season 3 has gone all in on Sprites. A little collectible you can track down in each game. Giving you unique Sprite powers. But also, something to extract and collect, so you can use them in the cosmetics in the Battle Plus. Plus, the bragging rights of just having a really rare sprite. So how do the Fortnite Cube Sprites work and when is their release date going to be? What are Fortnite Cube Sprites?

stories.title: Zapatero Faces Scrutiny Over €1.3 Million in Undeclared Jewels
stories.summary: Former Spanish Prime Minister José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero faces scrutiny over €1.3 million in undeclared jewels found in his office safe during a judicial search on May 19. Despite initially describing them as a "courtesy gift," Zapatero has yet to provide full details, raising concerns about potential ethical breaches and tax violations.

stories.title: Saudi prince found dead in £600-a-night London hotel room as cause of death unearthed
stories.summary: A Saudi prince who was found dead in a lavish Kensington hotel room was almost three times over the drink-drive limit and had "fatal levels" of the party drug GHP in his system, an inquest heard. Abdullah bin Fahad bin Abdullah bin Abdulaziz bin Jalawi al Saud was found dead in his £600-a-night Marriott hotel room in Kensington on the 25 November last year. A cleaner found the prince, 29, lying in the bathroom of his locked fifth-floor room fully clothed. Hotel security and ambulance were called but were unable to resuscitate him . He was sadly pronounced dead at the scene.
stories.trend: saudi prince died in london hotel

stories.title: TV tonight: documentary reveals the shocking truth behind model family blogger Ruby Franke
stories.summary: The Devil in the Family: The Fall of Ruby Franke 9pm, BBC Two At its peak, Ruby Franke’s parenting YouTube channel was earning £100,000 a month. But, as this documentary series shows, her ostensibly model family was a house of cards. As her ex-husband puts it: “The devil knocked and we opened the door. ” Their downfall culminated in Franke’s arrest for child abuse in 2023 after her emaciated son escaped and summoned help. Thanks to the plentiful footage recorded by Franke herself, it’s easy to sense the roots of this horror. Phil Harrison The Chase Around the World 9pm, ITV1 The shrewd expansion of the Chase universe into a travel contest with a general knowledge quiz attached continues.

stories.title: The Game Takes a Bottle to the Head at 'VERZUZ' With YG
stories.summary: The Game temporarily turned a concert into a drinking contest during his VERZUZ event with YG , downing almost an entire bottle of tequila in a single gulp. During the event on Thursday (July 23), the Compton (or, as he repeatedly said it throughout the evening, “Bompton”) rapper took a giant swallow of what looked like Don Julio while onstage, and dropped the empty bottle to the ground in celebration when he was done. Don’t believe us? See it for yourself below. This VERZUZ matchup pits two generations of Compton against each other. The Game, born Jayceon Terrell Taylor, broke out as a Dr.

stories.title: Walla Walla Iris Society Annual Rhizome Sale
stories.summary: WALLA - Rhonda and Charles Nosler of Waitsburg have been members of the Walla Iris Society since 2019 and currently have over 150 iris varieties. The WWIS will hold its annual sale at the Milton-Freewater Community Building on August 1 and 2 from 9 a. m. to 5 p. m. Irises will be presented by name, with variety information and a photo. For newcomers to growing irises, planting and care instructions will be available. The sale will include tall, intermediate, border, miniature tall, and standard dwarf varieties of bearded irises. Bearded irises are perennials that grow well in the Walla Valley, with its hot summers, cold winters, and well-drained soil.

stories.title: Ajax and Racing want Jofre Torrents as Barcelona teen set for season-long loan
stories.summary: Barcelona left-back Jofre Torrents looks to be heading out of the club this summer on a season-long loan to gain some first-team experience. According to Cadena SER, Ajax and Racing are two clubs hoping to land the 19-year-old for the 2026-26 campaign. Torrents looked set to break into the first-team last season after featuring in pre-season but suffered a serious ankle injury in February that kept him sidelined for months. Barca think the best move now is for the teenager to continue his development by playing regularly. Ajax is a potential destination, along with Racing and another unnamed Spanish club.

stories.title: 'Evidence lacking' for mental health monitoring tech
stories.summary: The Lampard Inquiry hears from experts about Oxevision technology. There is no "strong independent evidence" that a monitoring system used widely in mental health wards has improved patient safety, an inquiry has heard. The Lampard Inquiry is examining the deaths of more than 2,000 mental health patients in Essex over a 24-year period. The inquiry had previously heard "grave concerns" expressed about the widespread use of Oxevision technology , which monitors patients in their rooms using infrared sensors and cameras.

stories.title: Death of husband in HSE care leaves unanswered questions
stories.summary: The wife of a man who took his own life while receiving psychiatric treatment in Cork has questioned how he was able to do so while he was being held in a secure mental health unit. 67-year-old Mark Reilly was admitted to Cork University Hospital's Acute Mental Health Unit in March 2022 because he was suicidal. Cork University Hospital and the HSE have apologised for failings in Mark's care. Nancy Reilly said Mark, a former software engineer, community volunteer and teacher, had shown no signs of mental illness during their 37-year marriage before developing what she believes was a psychosis from late 2021.

stories.title: US imposes tariffs on dozens of trade partners, citing forced labour concerns
stories.summary: The US has imposed new tariffs of 10% to 12.5% on imports from 60 trading partners, including the UK, China, the EU, Canada, Japan and India, citing forced labour concerns. The duties took effect on Friday after a temporary 10% tax on foreign goods expired earlier this year. The move follows a Supreme Court ruling that previous tariffs were unlawful and reflects the Trump administration’s renewed push to enforce trade restrictions.

stories.title: A Million-Panel Project
stories.summary: Utah’s new solar power and battery storage facility is among the largest in the region. 3 min read Image of the Day for July 24, 2026 Utah’s new solar power and battery storage facility is among the largest in the region. NASA Earth Observatory Jul 24, 2026 Article View more Images of the Day: Historically, central Utah’s Castle Valley has been a coal hub, with mining operations on the slopes of the Wasatch Plateau to the west active since the late 1800s. A different energy development arrived in the region in June 2026, when a large solar power and battery storage plant came online in the sunny valley about 130 miles (210 kilometers) southeast of Salt Lake City.

stories.title: Yandex introduces an AI that answers calls and makes bookings
stories.summary: The Russian technology company Yandex has developed a new AI agent specialized in handling user calls and making bookings for various services. This innovative project is expected to be a new milestone in automating customer communication for entrepreneurs in the service sector. This is reported by Ixbt. com reports . The new "AI-hostess" system can communicate in natural language with customers calling restaurants, cafes, or other service venues. According to ixbt. com, the virtual assistant continues to accept customer requests even when staff are busy or after business hours. This allows business owners to avoid losing potential customers.

stories.title: ‘Sia: Nostalgic for the Present’ Review: A Concert Performance Film from 2016, but It Channels Sia’s Mystery and Power
stories.summary: Sia’s greatest songs, like "Unstoppable" and "Titanium" and "The Greatest," are anthems of strength, power, fortitude, invincibility, and watching the movie it’s fascinating to behold how her facelessness feeds that quality. She’s an abstract figure, a pair of lips singing, and this creates the sensation that the power she’s singing about doesn’t emanate from her. She’s channeling a force larger than herself — the rising specter of feminine triumph. She’s merely the vessel.

stories.title: Brent Crude Oil Prices Top $100 as Conflict With Iran Drags On
stories.summary: Stocks in Asia were down following a sell-off in the U.S. markets over soaring oil prices, rising borrowing costs and worries about the technology industry.

stories.title: Trump Administration Imposed New Tariffs of Around 10% on Over 80 Nations
stories.summary: The administration launched new duties tied to countries’ imports of goods made with forced labor, replacing a 10 percent tariff immediately after it expired.

stories.title: Burnham urged to 'build bridges' with sacked Starmerites
stories.summary: Andy Burnham needs to "build bridges" with the Starmerite minister he sacked during his reshuffle, Baroness Harriet Harman has told the Electoral Dysfunction podcast.

stories.title: Trump’s Global Tariffs: What’s the Latest on the Trade War
stories.summary: The president has unveiled a complicated system to forge ahead with his trade war, after the Supreme Court stuck down his original levies.
entities: Supreme Court of the United States

stories.title: Burnham to spend first day working from northern branch of No 10
stories.summary: Andy Burnham will hold his first meeting at the new No 10 North office in Manchester today, marking a push to decentralise government power. Up to 300 civil servants will relocate to the site by the end of next year as part of plans to shift decision-making away from London. The prime minister will chair a National Economic Council meeting to prioritise regional devolution and local growth.

stories.title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4954–4960: Celebrating Our Rover Engineers Past and Present
stories.summary: 3 min read Mars Science Laboratory Mission Team Members Jul 23, 2026 Article Written by Lucy Thompson, Senior Research Scientist, University of New Brunswick, Canada Earth planning date: Friday, July 27, 2026 As an APXS uplink lead and strategic planner, I have the privilege of working with the rover engineers most days that I am on operations. The APXS instrument measures the chemistry of rocks, unconsolidated materials and the atmosphere, and is situated on the end of Curiosity’s robotic arm. This means that any target of interest that we wish to analyze has to be safe to deploy the arm, APXS, and MAHLI (the closeup imager) to.

stories.title: Italian Police Union Defends Officers After Killing of Abderrahim Fakir
stories.summary: Rabat- The Italian Police Workers' Union (Sindacato Italiano Unitario Lavoratori Polizia, SIULP) has defended the officers involved in the police intervention that resulted in the killing of Moroccan migrant Abderrahim Fakir in Bologna, even as the case continues to fuel public outrage, protests, and calls for accountability in Italy and Morocco.

stories.title: Indonesian babies were trafficked to Singapore. What happens to them now?
stories.summary: At least 12 babies have been trafficked from Indonesia to Singapore, but what will happen to them remains unclear. Indonesian babies were trafficked to Singapore. What happens to them now? Figure caption, Watch: A baby trafficking ring has been convicted - but what now for the babies? By Tessa Wong Reporting from Singapore and Astudestra Ajengrastri Reporting from Jakarta On Tuesday an Indonesian court convicted 19 people for baby trafficking, wrapping up a high-profile case - but leaving a key question unanswered. The members of the trafficking syndicate were found guilty of procuring at least 34 babies and selling them for adoption.

stories.title: What to Know About Peptides the FDA Is Considering for Use
stories.summary: The agency is considering whether compounding pharmacies should be allowed to produce BPC-157, TB-500 and other previously banned peptides.

stories.title: Littler cruises into Matchplay semis with win over Rock
stories.summary: World number one Luke Littler's World Matchplay title defence continues with a 16-7 quarter-final win over Josh Rock in Blackpool, as he averages 109. 57. Luke Littler's World Matchplay title defence continued with a straightforward 16-7 quarter-final win over Josh Rock in Blackpool. Rock initially kept pace with the world number one as the players traded breaks to reach 5-5. But after 19-year-old Littler reeled off five legs in a row, there proved to be no way back for Rock as the defending champion, in dazzling form, cruised into the semi-finals. After throwing 17 180s with a scoring average of 109. 57, Littler said: "At the minute, everything's going to plan.

stories.title: Google fined €890m by EU for favouring its own apps over rivals
stories.summary: Google has been fined €890 million by the European Commission for abusing its market dominance by favouring its own apps over rivals. The penalty, the first under the EU’s Digital Markets Act, follows two breaches where Google gave its services an unfair advantage. Google warns the ruling could strip key features from its European services.

stories.title: Judge Extends Pause for Paramount-Warner Bros. Deal
stories.summary: The court blocked the companies from closing the acquisition through mid-August, as a judge considers two lawsuits challenging it.

stories.title: King's Tardis entrance as Commonwealth Games begin
stories.summary: King Charles III emerges from a Tardis and a Loch Ness Monster takes the stage at the Commonwealth Games opening ceremony in Glasgow. One of the louder cheers came for the Falklands Islands – in the news recently following Argentina's victory over England in the football World Cup semi-finals – and another followed when the Samoa team emerged and performed a haka in front of the dignitaries in the Royal Box. But nothing compared to the noise which greeted the arrival of the Scotland team, who entered the arena last in their white shirts and kilts and lifted many in the capacity crowd from their seats.

stories.title: Number of U.S. Troop Deaths in Iran War Appears Lower on Pentagon Website
stories.summary: Three military officials and a Pentagon spokesman gave conflicting explanations for why the number of Americans killed in the Iran war had changed.

stories.title: FDA Panel Supports Lifting Restrictions on Four Peptides
stories.summary: In an all-day hearing, the agency’s scientists argued against allowing broad access to unproven drugs that the agency had banned three years ago.

stories.title: Amid Cyclospora Outbreak Tied to Lettuce, America Skips Salads
stories.summary: Foot traffic to chains such as Chopt and Chipotle fell in recent weeks after a recall from Taylor Farms and changing messages from the F.D.A.

stories.title: Hull KR inflict more derby misery on Hull FC
stories.summary: Hull KR beat local rivals Hull FC 28-20 in a tense Super League derby. The Robins, coached by Willie Peters, moved into the play-off spots with nine straight derby wins. Hull FC suffered their sixth straight loss, extending their winless run to just one victory in their last 11 games.

stories.title: Health Care Agency Discontinues Dozens of Active Research Awards
stories.summary: The Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality slowed the awarding of new grants last year. Now it is stopping existing ones.
entities: Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality

Cork University Hospital and the HSE have apologised for failings in Mark's care. Nancy Reilly said Mark, a former software engineer, community volunteer and teacher, had shown no signs of mental illness during their 37-year marriage before developing what she believes was a psychosis from late 2021.

offbeat.title: Yandex introduces an AI that answers calls and makes bookings
offbeat.summary: The Russian technology company Yandex has developed a new AI agent specialized in handling user calls and making bookings for various services. This innovative project is expected to be a new milestone in automating customer communication for entrepreneurs in the service sector. This is reported by Ixbt. com reports . The new "AI-hostess" system can communicate in natural language with customers calling restaurants, cafes, or other service venues. According to ixbt. com, the virtual assistant continues to accept customer requests even when staff are busy or after business hours. This allows business owners to avoid losing potential customers.

offbeat.title: Italian Police Union Defends Officers After Killing of Abderrahim Fakir
offbeat.summary: Rabat- The Italian Police Workers' Union (Sindacato Italiano Unitario Lavoratori Polizia, SIULP) has defended the officers involved in the police intervention that resulted in the killing of Moroccan migrant Abderrahim Fakir in Bologna, even as the case continues to fuel public outrage, protests, and calls for accountability in Italy and Morocco.

offbeat.title: Indonesian babies were trafficked to Singapore. What happens to them now?
offbeat.summary: At least 12 babies have been trafficked from Indonesia to Singapore, but what will happen to them remains unclear. Indonesian babies were trafficked to Singapore. What happens to them now? Figure caption, Watch: A baby trafficking ring has been convicted - but what now for the babies? By Tessa Wong Reporting from Singapore and Astudestra Ajengrastri Reporting from Jakarta On Tuesday an Indonesian court convicted 19 people for baby trafficking, wrapping up a high-profile case - but leaving a key question unanswered. The members of the trafficking syndicate were found guilty of procuring at least 34 babies and selling them for adoption.

health.title: 'Evidence lacking' for mental health monitoring tech
health.summary: The Lampard Inquiry hears from experts about Oxevision technology. There is no "strong independent evidence" that a monitoring system used widely in mental health wards has improved patient safety, an inquiry has heard. The Lampard Inquiry is examining the deaths of more than 2,000 mental health patients in Essex over a 24-year period. The inquiry had previously heard "grave concerns" expressed about the widespread use of Oxevision technology , which monitors patients in their rooms using infrared sensors and cameras.

health.title: FDA Panel Supports Lifting Restrictions on Four Peptides
health.summary: In an all-day hearing, the agency’s scientists argued against allowing broad access to unproven drugs that the agency had banned three years ago.

health.title: Health Care Agency Discontinues Dozens of Active Research Awards
health.summary: The Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality slowed the awarding of new grants last year. Now it is stopping existing ones.
entities: Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality

breaking.title: US imposes tariffs on dozens of trade partners, citing forced labour concerns
breaking.summary: The US has imposed new tariffs of 10% to 12.5% on imports from 60 trading partners, including the UK, China, the EU, Canada, Japan and India, citing forced labour concerns. The duties took effect on Friday after a temporary 10% tax on foreign goods expired earlier this year. The move follows a Supreme Court ruling that previous tariffs were unlawful and reflects the Trump administration’s renewed push to enforce trade restrictions.

facts.title: A Million-Panel Project
facts.summary: Utah’s new solar power and battery storage facility is among the largest in the region. 3 min read Image of the Day for July 24, 2026 Utah’s new solar power and battery storage facility is among the largest in the region. NASA Earth Observatory Jul 24, 2026 Article View more Images of the Day: Historically, central Utah’s Castle Valley has been a coal hub, with mining operations on the slopes of the Wasatch Plateau to the west active since the late 1800s. A different energy development arrived in the region in June 2026, when a large solar power and battery storage plant came online in the sunny valley about 130 miles (210 kilometers) southeast of Salt Lake City.

facts.title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4954–4960: Celebrating Our Rover Engineers Past and Present
facts.summary: 3 min read Mars Science Laboratory Mission Team Members Jul 23, 2026 Article Written by Lucy Thompson, Senior Research Scientist, University of New Brunswick, Canada Earth planning date: Friday, July 27, 2026 As an APXS uplink lead and strategic planner, I have the privilege of working with the rover engineers most days that I am on operations. The APXS instrument measures the chemistry of rocks, unconsolidated materials and the atmosphere, and is situated on the end of Curiosity’s robotic arm. This means that any target of interest that we wish to analyze has to be safe to deploy the arm, APXS, and MAHLI (the closeup imager) to.

facts.title: What to Know About Peptides the FDA Is Considering for Use
facts.summary: The agency is considering whether compounding pharmacies should be allowed to produce BPC-157, TB-500 and other previously banned peptides.

entertainment.title: ‘Sia: Nostalgic for the Present’ Review: A Concert Performance Film from 2016, but It Channels Sia’s Mystery and Power
entertainment.summary: Sia’s greatest songs, like "Unstoppable" and "Titanium" and "The Greatest," are anthems of strength, power, fortitude, invincibility, and watching the movie it’s fascinating to behold how her facelessness feeds that quality. She’s an abstract figure, a pair of lips singing, and this creates the sensation that the power she’s singing about doesn’t emanate from her. She’s channeling a force larger than herself — the rising specter of feminine triumph. She’s merely the vessel.

tech.title: Brent Crude Oil Prices Top $100 as Conflict With Iran Drags On
tech.summary: Stocks in Asia were down following a sell-off in the U.S. markets over soaring oil prices, rising borrowing costs and worries about the technology industry.

tech.title: Google fined €890m by EU for favouring its own apps over rivals
tech.summary: Google has been fined €890 million by the European Commission for abusing its market dominance by favouring its own apps over rivals. The penalty, the first under the EU’s Digital Markets Act, follows two breaches where Google gave its services an unfair advantage. Google warns the ruling could strip key features from its European services.

tech.title: Judge Extends Pause for Paramount-Warner Bros. Deal
tech.summary: The court blocked the companies from closing the acquisition through mid-August, as a judge considers two lawsuits challenging it.

crisis.title: Trump Administration Imposed New Tariffs of Around 10% on Over 80 Nations
crisis.summary: The administration launched new duties tied to countries’ imports of goods made with forced labor, replacing a 10 percent tariff immediately after it expired.

crisis.title: Trump’s Global Tariffs: What’s the Latest on the Trade War
crisis.summary: The president has unveiled a complicated system to forge ahead with his trade war, after the Supreme Court stuck down his original levies.
entities: Supreme Court of the United States

crisis.title: Amid Cyclospora Outbreak Tied to Lettuce, America Skips Salads
crisis.summary: Foot traffic to chains such as Chopt and Chipotle fell in recent weeks after a recall from Taylor Farms and changing messages from the F.D.A.

politics.title: Burnham urged to 'build bridges' with sacked Starmerites
politics.summary: Andy Burnham needs to "build bridges" with the Starmerite minister he sacked during his reshuffle, Baroness Harriet Harman has told the Electoral Dysfunction podcast.

politics.title: Burnham to spend first day working from northern branch of No 10
politics.summary: Andy Burnham will hold his first meeting at the new No 10 North office in Manchester today, marking a push to decentralise government power. Up to 300 civil servants will relocate to the site by the end of next year as part of plans to shift decision-making away from London. The prime minister will chair a National Economic Council meeting to prioritise regional devolution and local growth.

politics.title: Number of U.S. Troop Deaths in Iran War Appears Lower on Pentagon Website
politics.summary: Three military officials and a Pentagon spokesman gave conflicting explanations for why the number of Americans killed in the Iran war had changed.

sports.title: Littler cruises into Matchplay semis with win over Rock
sports.summary: World number one Luke Littler's World Matchplay title defence continues with a 16-7 quarter-final win over Josh Rock in Blackpool, as he averages 109. 57. Luke Littler's World Matchplay title defence continued with a straightforward 16-7 quarter-final win over Josh Rock in Blackpool. Rock initially kept pace with the world number one as the players traded breaks to reach 5-5. But after 19-year-old Littler reeled off five legs in a row, there proved to be no way back for Rock as the defending champion, in dazzling form, cruised into the semi-finals. After throwing 17 180s with a scoring average of 109. 57, Littler said: "At the minute, everything's going to plan.

sports.title: King's Tardis entrance as Commonwealth Games begin
sports.summary: King Charles III emerges from a Tardis and a Loch Ness Monster takes the stage at the Commonwealth Games opening ceremony in Glasgow. One of the louder cheers came for the Falklands Islands – in the news recently following Argentina's victory over England in the football World Cup semi-finals – and another followed when the Samoa team emerged and performed a haka in front of the dignitaries in the Royal Box. But nothing compared to the noise which greeted the arrival of the Scotland team, who entered the arena last in their white shirts and kilts and lifted many in the capacity crowd from their seats.

sports.title: Hull KR inflict more derby misery on Hull FC
sports.summary: Hull KR beat local rivals Hull FC 28-20 in a tense Super League derby. The Robins, coached by Willie Peters, moved into the play-off spots with nine straight derby wins. Hull FC suffered their sixth straight loss, extending their winless run to just one victory in their last 11 games.
